requirement.original: Refactor the /allow bypass system to fix 6 architectural issues identified by the architect. Scope constraint (non-negotiable): the set of operations bypassable via /allow must remain exactly identical to today's.
requirement.clarified: Fix allow-1 through allow-6 as architect described. allow-1: remove dead sid variable. allow-2: centralise allowlist check in dispatcher (push exempt). allow-3: remove 6 dead per-rule callsites in bash hook. allow-4: no code change needed. allow-5: move IS_SUBAGENT assignment to top of bash hook to eliminate duplicate inline parse. allow-6: Won't Have — consolidation not feasible this cycle.
requirement.what: Structural refactoring of pretool-git-privilege-guard.py and pretool-bash-safety.sh to eliminate dead code, add structural enforcement, and remove duplicate computation
requirement.why: 6 architectural issues degrade maintainability, create silent omission risk (allow-2), and cause reader confusion (allow-3). allow-1 is dead code. allow-5 is a duplicate subprocess invocation per bypass check.

included: hooks/pretool-git-privilege-guard.py — M1 (dead sid), M2 (central fast-path allowlist check for non-push; per-function calls retained)

hooks/pretool-bash-safety.sh — M3 (remove 6 secondary calls + stale comment at lines ~690-696), M4 (IS_SUBAGENT relocation, remove inline parse from check_and_consume_allowlist, remove _DO_IS_SUB inline parse at /do bypass block lines ~600-602, update stale comment at lines ~124-131)
excluded: hooks/lib/allowlist.py — allow-6 consolidation is Won't Have this cycle

out_of_path_observed_but_not_touched: hooks/lib/allowlist.py — grant-read implementation; allow-6 consolidation deferred

out_of_scope_observations.observation: allow-6: there are FOUR independent grant-read/match implementations: (1) hooks/lib/allowlist.py:read_grant — tool_name match; (2) pretool-git-privilege-guard.py:_check_git_allowlist — command-string match; (3) pretool-bash-safety.sh heredoc — compound-command split + SIGALRM + flock; (4) posttool-allowlist-consume.py — independent Python implement of compound-command split + pattern match + os.unlink (does NOT use lib/allowlist.py; imports only lib.subagent). Consolidation not feasible this cycle — bash heredoc requires non-trivial CLI entry point work; command-string vs tool-name semantics differ. Deferred to a dedicated future cycle.

root_cause_analysis.symptom: 6 architectural issues in the /allow bypass system: dead variable, unstructured per-function enforcement, dead secondary callsites, confirmed-correct item, duplicate IS_SUBAGENT parse, and 3 independent grant-read implementations
root_cause_analysis.root_cause: Incremental additions to the bypass system over multiple cycles without a centralised refactoring pass. Each new evaluate-* function was added with a copy-paste of the _check_git_allowlist call; the global short-circuit in pretool-bash-safety.sh was added later without removing the per-rule calls; IS_SUBAGENT was added at the bottom of the script for a specific use case without reorganising the earlier function that needed the same check.
root_cause_analysis.root_cause_commit: N/A — not a regression; accumulated architectural debt
root_cause_analysis.why_introduced: Correct security additions made in isolation under time pressure; no refactoring pass followed
root_cause_analysis.why_problematic: allow-2: new evaluate-* functions silently miss the bypass check. allow-3: structural inconsistency confuses readers about whether per-rule calls are load-bearing. allow-5: duplicate subprocess per bypass check (performance + drift risk).
root_cause_analysis.timeline: Current state — no regression, pure architectural debt
observations: sid = _get_session_id(data) at pretool-git-privilege-guard.py:658 — assigned, never read in _evaluate_command scope (inferred: static code reading)
_check_git_allowlist calls at lines 455, 480, 583, 602, 615, 648 — 6 per-function calls with no dispatcher-level enforcement (inferred: static code reading)

check_and_consume_allowlist at line 615 fires unconditionally before any block rule; secondary calls at 805, 984, 995, 1007, 1027, 1110 have identical pattern-match semantics with same input — dead code (inferred: static code reading + logic trace)
IS_SUBAGENT assignment at line 1068 is after line 615 where check_and_consume_allowlist is called; inner parse at lines 142-147 is structurally identical — duplicate (inferred: static code reading)

OBJ-7 partial (measured — iteration 2): main() at line 684 passes the RAW full bash string to _evaluate_command without splitting. _check_git_allowlist receives and pattern-matches against this full string. For a compound command 'git push origin main; git commit -m x', both _looks_like_git_push and _looks_like_git_commit match the full string. When an /allow grant is present, the per-function commit check yields the same outcome as the push check (same pattern, same full string — both match). OBJ-7's claim that adding return destroys independent per-subcommand protection is incorrect for the /allow grant path.

OBJ-8 (genuine regression — confirmed by QA and Codex, iteration 3): the iteration 2 M2 structure with `return` after _evaluate_push introduced a real regression on the CLAUDE_PUSH_COMMAND_ACTIVE path. When CLAUDE_PUSH_COMMAND_ACTIVE=1 allows push without a grant, _check_git_allowlist in _evaluate_commit returns False (no grant), and default-deny fires. The `return` after push caused _evaluate_commit to never run, suppressing the default-deny for the commit subcommand. OBJ-7 analysis covered only the /allow grant sub-path; it did not cover the CLAUDE_PUSH_COMMAND_ACTIVE no-grant path. Fix: add central fast-path call conditioned on `not _looks_like_git_push`, retain all per-function calls, do NOT add return after _evaluate_push. This is the revised M2 structure (iteration 3).
root_cause_analysis.diagnosis_completeness: complete (has both inferred and measured observations; OBJ-7 partial rebuttal in iteration 2; OBJ-8 genuine regression acknowledged and fix confirmed in iteration 3)
root_cause_analysis.localization_evidence: static code reading; dead-code claims are verifiable by control-flow trace

development_approach.validation_approach: Static inspection: grep confirms removal of dead sid. grep confirms _evaluate_command contains 'not _looks_like_git_push' central fast-path call. grep confirms per-function _check_git_allowlist calls are RETAINED in _evaluate_commit, _evaluate_merge, _evaluate_reset_hard, _evaluate_direct_ref_mutation, _evaluate_forbidden_plumbing (5 calls present). grep confirms no `return` immediately follows `_evaluate_push(command, data)` inside the `_looks_like_git_push` branch of _evaluate_command. grep confirms _evaluate_push still contains _check_git_allowlist call after _push_has_forbidden_ref_mutation. grep confirms 6 secondary check_and_consume_allowlist calls removed from pretool-bash-safety.sh. grep confirms IS_SUBAGENT assigned in first 30 lines of bash script. grep confirms no remaining agent_id inline parse outside the single IS_SUBAGENT= assignment. grep confirms _DO_IS_SUB inline parse replaced. Scope invariant: trace _evaluate_push — _push_has_forbidden_ref_mutation is still before _check_git_allowlist. Verify stale comment at lines ~690-696 is removed/rewritten. AC8 scenario trace: command='git push origin main; git commit -m x', CLAUDE_PUSH_COMMAND_ACTIVE=1, push grant present, no /allow/commit grant — confirm default-deny fires for commit.

research_findings.reason: Straightforward architectural refactoring of existing security hooks with well-understood code. No new patterns or architectures introduced. Root cause and fix are clear from static code reading.
